lede: A look at March 10 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Matt Poitras and Ivan Rakitić, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: Bam Adebayo
events.desc: Bam Adebayo of the Miami Heat recorded the second-highest scoring game in NBA history with 83 Points.

events.title: Ethiopian Airlines Flight 302
events.desc: Ethiopian Airlines Flight 302, a Boeing 737 MAX, crashes shortly after takeoff, killing all 157 passengers and crew. This and the prior Lion Air Flight 610 led to all 387 Boeing 737 MAX aircraft being grounded worldwide.

events.title: Syzygy (astronomy)
events.desc: Syzygy: All nine planets recognized at this time — Mercury to Pluto — align on the same side of the Sun.

events.title: 1975 spring offensive
events.desc: Vietnam War: Ho Chi Minh Campaign: North Vietnamese troops attack Ban Mê Thuột in the South on their way to capturing Saigon in the final push for victory over South Vietnam.

events.title: Vietnam War
events.desc: Vietnam War: Captain Ernest Medina is charged by the U.S. military with My Lai war crimes.

events.title: 1959 Tibetan uprising
events.desc: Tibetan uprising: Fearing an abduction attempt by China, thousands of Tibetans surround the Dalai Lama's palace to prevent his removal.

events.title: Mildred Gillars
events.desc: Mildred Gillars ("Axis Sally") is convicted of treason.

events.title: World War II
events.desc: World War II: The U.S. Army Air Force firebombs Tokyo, and the resulting conflagration kills more than 100,000 people, mostly civilians.

events.title: Mahatma Gandhi
events.desc: Mahatma Gandhi is arrested in India, tried for sedition, and sentenced to six years in prison, only to be released after nearly two years for an appendicitis operation.

events.title: Napoleon
events.desc: Emperor Napoleon I is defeated at the Battle of Laon in France.

births.name: Madhavrao Scindia
births.line: Indian politician, Indian Minister of Railways (died 2001)

births.name: James Earl Ray
births.line: American criminal; assassin of Martin Luther King Jr. (died 1998)

births.name: Joseph Légaré
births.line: Canadian painter and glazier, artist, seigneur and political figure (died 1855)

deaths.text: Stanley R. Jaffe, American film producer and director
F. Sherwood Rowland, American chemist and academic, Nobel Prize laureate
Frank O'Connor, Irish short story writer, novelist, and poet
Carl Reinecke, German pianist, composer, and conductor
Élie Catherine Fréron, French author and critic
